description: GitHub Actions OIDC subjects against classic Azure federated-identity-credential (FIC) subjects. Classic FIC is an EXACT, wildcard-free string match; a mismatch fails the token exchange silently with no error. Seed tranche: exact match, case-sensitivity, the pull_request over-permission CKV_AZURE_249 passes, the wildcard-as-literal trap (opposite of AWS), tag/environment scoping, and the classic-vs-immutable silent break.

vectors.description: FIC subject exactly equals the token sub for one branch of one repo. The tight baseline.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Exact match on a single branch of a single repo.

vectors.description: Classic FIC requires the subject to exactly match the token sub. A case difference in the owner (here 'Octo-Org' vs 'octo-org') does not match, and the exchange fails silently with no error.
vectors.subject: repo:Octo-Org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

vectors.description: A FIC whose subject is exactly the pull_request subject matches pull_request-triggered tokens. CKV_AZURE_249 PASSES this configuration; it admits code from pull requests (potentially forks).
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:pull_request

judgment.reason: The FIC admits the pull_request subject, so pull-request code (potentially from a fork) can assume this identity. Checkov CKV_AZURE_249 does not flag it.

vectors.description: The wildcard trap, opposite to AWS. An admin writes 'repo:octo-org/*' as a FIC subject expecting org-wide access as they would get from an AWS StringLike condition. Classic FIC does not support wildcards, so '*' is a literal character: this subject matches NO real token and the credential silently never authenticates. Fails closed (not over-permissive) but is a portability and correctness trap.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

vectors.description: FIC subject scoped to a named deployment environment. Matches only that environment's tokens.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:environment:Production

judgment.reason: Environment scoping is only as strong as the environment's protection rules; safe if the environment has required reviewers/branch restrictions, weaker otherwise.

vectors.description: Immutable subject (embedded owner/repo IDs) matched by a FIC configured in the same immutable form.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org@123456/octo-repo@456789:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Exact match on an immutable subject; the embedded IDs pin the identity across renames.

vectors.description: The July 15 break, Azure side, and nastier than AWS because it is silent. A repo created after the immutable cutover mints an immutable subject, but the FIC still carries the classic (no-@id) subject. Exact match fails, and per Microsoft the exchange fails with NO error -- deployments simply stop authenticating with no diagnostic.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org@123456/octo-repo@456789:ref:refs/heads/main

vectors.description: A different branch of the same repo does not match an exact FIC subject.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/feature-x

vectors.description: FIC subject scoped to a tag ref. Matches the tag token, but tags are movable and unprotected by default.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/tags/v1.0

judgment.reason: Tag refs are movable and, unless tag protection rules are configured, a maintainer or compromised token can repoint the tag to attacker-controlled code.

vectors.description: Exact match is byte-for-byte: a trailing space in the FIC subject means it never matches the token sub. Another silent-failure footgun.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main
